concept.name: repeat(auto-fill, minmax())
concept.explain:
Use repeat(auto-fill, minmax()) to create responsive columns that automatically fill the grid.

Example:
grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fill, minmax(80px, 1fr));

Each column will be at least 80px wide and expand to fill the available space.

ui.prompt: Goal: Keep the car in column 1 / row 2 and resize the columns using auto-fill.
hints: Each column should be at least 80px wide.

entities.baseCss: background: rgba(200,0,0,0.22); outline:2px dashed rgba(200,0,0,0.8); border-radius:10px; min-width:80px; justify-self:stretch;

concept.name: auto-fill rows
concept.explain:
auto-fill can also be used for rows.

Example:
grid-template-rows: repeat(auto-fill, minmax(80px, 1fr));

This creates rows that automatically fill the available space.

ui.prompt: Goal: Keep the car in column 3 / row 1 and create responsive rows using auto-fill.
hints: Each row should be at least 80px tall.

entities.baseCss: background: rgba(200,0,0,0.22); outline:2px dashed rgba(200,0,0,0.8); border-radius:10px; min-height:80px; align-self:stretch;

concept.name: auto-fit
concept.explain:
auto-fit works like auto-fill but collapses empty tracks.

Example:
grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fit, minmax(80px, 1fr));

This allows the tracks to expand and fill the grid when space is available.

ui.prompt: Goal: Keep the car in column 2 / row 2 and make the grid responsive using auto-fit.
hints: Both rows and columns should adapt to the container.

entities.baseCss: background: rgba(200,0,0,0.22); outline:2px dashed rgba(200,0,0,0.8); border-radius:10px; min-width:80px; min-height:80px; justify-self:stretch; align-self:stretch;
